decks.notes: The main strategy of this deck is to quickly ramp up mana using cards like Lotus Petal, City of Traitors, and Ancient Tomb in order to cheat massive creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Griselbrand into play through the use of Show and Tell and Sneak Attack. These creatures can quickly close out games by overwhelming the opponent with their powerful abilities. The deck also includes various control elements like Force of Will and Flusterstorm to protect its combo pieces and disrupt the opponent's plans. The goal is to win by executing the combo efficiently and effectively.

This deck focuses on utilizing powerful control cards such as Force of Will, Swords to Plowshares, and Teferi, Time Raveler, to disrupt opponents while generating card advantage through Brainstorm and Ponder. Quantum Riddler and Leyline Binding serve as additional disruption to control the board, while cards like Up the Beanstalk and Solitude provide removal and protection. The deck also includes a small ramp element with cards like Xander's Lounge and Ketria Triome to help stabilize and eventually overwhelm opponents with powerful threats like Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury. Additionally, the inclusion of versatile lands like Karakas and Mystic Sanctuary aids in controlling the battlefield.

This deck focuses on using efficient tempo plays and disruptive cards to control the game and put pressure on the opponent. It includes a mix of powerful creatures like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Murktide Regent, along with removal spells like Fatal Push and Snuff Out to deal with threats. The deck also uses disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Daze to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The goal is to maintain board presence and disrupt the opponent's strategy while quickly closing out the game with aggressive creatures and efficient spells.

The deck focuses on utilizing the Doomsday combo to quickly assemble and win the game. It includes powerful disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Force of Will to protect the combo, along with cantrips like Ponder and Brainstorm to filter through the deck. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provides additional card advantage and Thassa's Oracle serves as the win condition once the combo is set up. Additional acceleration in the form of Dark Ritual and Lion's Eye Diamond help speed up the process, while cards like Street Wraith and Murktide Regent offer alternate win conditions. The deck also includes Wasteland to disrupt opponents' mana bases.

The deck focuses on utilizing Cloudpost lands and ramping up mana quickly with cards like Ancient Tomb and Grim Monolith to cast powerful Eldrazi creatures like Thought-Knot Seer and Ulamog, the Ceaseless Hunger. The key strategy is to disrupt opponents with cards like Chalice of the Void and Trinisphere while also controlling the game with Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, the Eye of the Storms. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with its powerful creatures and disruptive spells, ultimately leading to a dominant board position and victory.

This Dimir Tempo deck focuses on disrupting opponents with counterspells like Force of Will and Daze, while efficiently controlling the board with removal spells like Fatal Push and Snuff Out. The deck also incorporates powerful threats like Murktide Regent and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares to pressure opponents and close out games quickly. Additionally, the deck utilizes card draw and manipulation with Brainstorm and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student to ensure consistency and adaptability in various situations. Overall, the deck aims to maintain tempo and disrupt opponents' game plans to secure victory.

This deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while establishing a fast and aggressive tempo. Using efficient removal spells like Fatal Push and Force of Will, alongside disruptive cards such as Thoughtseize and Daze, the deck aims to control the game early on. The creatures, like Murktide Regent and Brazen Borrower, provide strong threats to close out the game quickly. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Katara, Waterbending Master provide card advantage and additional ways to maintain pressure. The mana base is carefully crafted to support the deck's game plan and enable smooth gameplay.

This deck focuses on assembling the combo of Doomsday and Thassa's Oracle to win the game in one turn. The deck uses cards like Personal Tutor, Brainstorm, and Ponder to set up the combo efficiently, while also including disruptive cards like Force of Will and Thoughtseize to protect the combo. The deck also includes cards like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal to generate mana quickly, allowing for a fast and powerful win condition. The deck aims to carefully plan out its sequence of plays to maximize its chances of winning in a single turn.

This deck focuses on utilizing fast mana sources and rituals to generate a large amount of mana quickly, allowing the player to cast multiple spells in a single turn. The goal is to chain together spells that draw cards or generate mana to eventually cast Tendrils of Agony with a high storm count, dealing lethal damage to the opponent. The deck also includes various tools for protection and recursion, as well as Burning Wish to access additional cards from the sideboard to adapt to different situations.

The main idea of this deck is to disrupt your opponent's strategy while deploying aggressive threats quickly. By using cards like Thoughtseize, Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student, and Daze to disrupt your opponent's plans, while playing powerful creatures like Kaito, Bane of Nightmares and Murktide Regent, you can apply pressure and control the game tempo. Cards like Brainstorm and Ponder help you dig for the cards you need, while cards like Fatal Push and Force of Will help you deal with threats efficiently. Wasteland and Undercity Sewers provide additional disruption options.

The main focus of this Mardu Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and disruption spells. Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Amped Raptor provide powerful threats, while Thoughtseize and Cabal Therapy disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck also includes removal spells like Swords to Plowshares to deal with opposing threats. The mana base is designed to support the aggressive strategy, with a mix of dual lands and fetch lands to ensure consistent color access. Overall, the deck aims to apply early pressure and disrupt the opponent's plans to secure a quick victory.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly fill up the graveyard with a high number of cards and then win the game by casting Thassa's Oracle or Dread Return to bring back key creatures like Boggart Trawler or Fell the Profane. The deck utilizes a variety of spells and mana accelerants to achieve its combo win condition, aiming to overwhelm the opponent before they have a chance to respond. The inclusion of disruptive cards like Cabal Therapy and Pact of Negation helps protect the combo pieces and ensure a smooth execution of the deck's strategy.

The main focus of this deck is to maintain control over the game through disruption and removal spells such as Thoughtseize, Fatal Push, and Force of Will, while also applying pressure with aggressive creatures like Murktide Regent and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while maintaining a tempo advantage with cards like Daze and Brainstorm. The combination of disruption, removal, and aggression allows the deck to quickly take control of the game and outpace the opponent.

This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan while utilizing aggressive creatures and tempo plays to quickly close out the game. By playing disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Force of Will, along with removal spells like Fatal Push and Snuff Out, the deck aims to control the board and keep the opponent on the back foot. The aggressive creatures like Nethergoyf and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares put pressure on the opponent, while cards like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Brainstorm provide card advantage and consistency. The deck also utilizes land destruction with Wasteland to further disrupt the opponent's game plan.

The main concept of this deck is to maintain tempo and control the opponent's board while applying pressure with efficient creatures and disruptive spells. By utilizing cards like Brainstorm, Force of Will, and Daze to manipulate the hand and counter threats, along with removal spells like Fatal Push and Snuff Out, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and create openings for aggressive plays with creatures like Barrowgoyf and Murktide Regent. The mana base is streamlined for consistency and speed, allowing for a fast and proactive game plan.

The focus of this deck is to quickly put powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn onto the battlefield using cards like Sneak Attack and Show and Tell. These combo pieces are supported by cards like Force of Will and Spell Pierce to protect the combo from disruption. The deck also includes card draw spells like Brainstorm and Ponder to help find the necessary pieces. Overall, the strategy is to cheat out expensive creatures early in the game to overwhelm the opponent and secure victory.
